name: Xero
one_liner: Cloud accounting platform for small businesses whose API exposes invoices, contacts, bank transactions, payroll and financial reports as agent-callable operations.
access_notes: A free Xero account doubles as a developer account and comes with a fully-functional Demo Company populated with sample data. In the developer portal ('My apps') you instantly self-register an app to get a client ID/secret and OAuth2 credentials, then hit the API against demo data via the API Explorer. No payment, approval, or business verification is required to get working credentials. (App certification/marketplace listing is only needed to remove connection limits at scale, not to start building.)
api_notes: Large REST API family: Accounting API alone covers ~40+ resources (Invoices, Contacts, Accounts, BankTransactions, Payments, CreditNotes, Quotes, ManualJournals, Reports, etc.). Additional product APIs include Payroll (AU/UK/NZ), Files, Assets, Projects, Bank Feeds, and Finance API. Official SDKs (Python, .NET, Node, Java, PHP, Ruby). OAuth2 client-credentials flow also supported for custom single-tenant apps.
mcp_notes: Vendor ships an official MCP server under the XeroAPI GitHub org (@xeroapi/xero-mcp-server), part of Xero's AI Toolkit (developer.xero.com/ai). Uses OAuth2; tools for contacts, chart of accounts, invoices, credit notes, quotes, payments, bank transactions, manual journals, and payroll (NZ/UK). Runnable via npx and configurable in Claude Desktop.
main_blocker: None significant for building; production scale requires app certification to lift the uncertified-app connection limit, but development and testing are fully self-serve free.

evidence.claim: Xero API uses OAuth 2.0 with standard authorization code and client credentials flows
Free Xero account serves as a developer account with a free Demo Company; register an app in 'My apps' to get a client ID/secret instantly
Xero ships an official MCP server (@xeroapi/xero-mcp-server) under the XeroAPI org
OAuth2 auth flow and scopes documented on the developer portal

open_questions: Exact operation count across all product APIs not tallied; certification thresholds and rate limits for production apps not fully reviewed here.
